title: Error banner renders before the selected_chat branch so bootstrap errors are always visible
description: In the CentralPanel's update() body, the error banner (if let Some(err) = &self.state.error) is rendered before the if self.state.selected_chat.is_none() branch. This ordering is load-bearing: bootstrap errors — auth failure, no JMAP Chat account in the session — arrive before any chat is selected, so selected_chat is None when they fire. If the banner were inside the else branch (message panel), it would never render during bootstrap failure, leaving the UI silently stuck on 'Select a chat to start messaging' with no indication of what went wrong. The banner must be outside both branches so it renders regardless of selection state. A future contributor might move the banner inside the message scroll area for visual tidiness — that would hide bootstrap errors.
close_reason: Good decision already implemented in current codebase
